'''Adria Reyn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who, through [[Politics/Legends|political]] maneuvering and corrupt activities, was appointed [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Callia sector/Legends|Callia sector]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] reign. After [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] strengthened a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] around the Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Vaxal]] within the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], [[Operative|agents]] representing the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] [[Sassallo]], who had been operating on the planet, confronted Reyn and blackmailed her into opening a narrow gap within the planet's [[sensor net]] to allow the safe and undetected passage of slaver [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]. Though Reyn complied and was paid for her cooperation, she swore to break off her deal with Sassallo at the first possible chance.

==Biography==
Adria Reyn was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who [[Politics/Legends|politically]] maneuvered herself through the ranks of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to highly favorable positions of authority. After many illicit dealings, she was eventually appointed [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Callia sector/Legends|Callia sector]] of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]], home of the Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Vaxal]]. Prior to the rise of the Empire, the [[Oubliette (shadowport)|Oubliette]] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] on Ord Vaxal had been the site of an underground [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] ring<ref name="Poly " /> led by the Hutt [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sassallo]]. Though Sassallo was able to bypass the pre-existing [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] above Ord Vaxal, her operations came to a halt when [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] declared himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and increased security measures around the blockade, forcing the Hutt to turn to Moff Reyn.<ref name="Poly " />

Unbeknown to Reyn, some of the second parties she utilized in her more unlawful dealings were also employed to Sassallo. Shortly after the strengthening of the blockade, Reyn was confronted by Sassallo's [[operative]]s, who used their knowledge of the Moff's private exploits as leverage for negotiations concerning the blockade's tight security. Left with little choice, Reyn reluctantly allowed the slavers to blackmail her and, within [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], she secretly rearranged the [[sensor net]] around Ord Vaxal to allow for a narrow access corridor leading to the Oubliette shadowport for the slavers' use.<ref name="Poly " /> Despite being unhappy with the arrangement, Reyn was paid well by Sassallo for keeping the route open, and over the next few years the Moff expanded upon her position of authority within the [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="Poly " />

==Personality and traits==
Adria Reyn was a highly-skilled politician but possessed little practical military experience. She maneuvered her way up the Imperial ladder by any means possible and had no qualms about using bribery, extortion, slander, or murder to achieve her goals. She expected nothing less than perfection from her subordinates and those who failed to please her were often reassigned to less important but highly dangerous roles. Reyn lived only for the power and prestige that came with her position as Moff and was considered the perfect example of Imperial duplicity and corruption.<ref name="Poly " /> She was taken by surprise when Sassallo was able to undermine her so easily, but vowed to sever her ties with the Hutt if ever given the chance.<ref name="Poly " />

Reyn was a middle-aged woman during the early reign of the Empire with graying brown hair, cruel hazel [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and an athletic frame. Her [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]] and attire allowed her to radiate an aura of intimidation but maintain her femininity at the same time. She was typically armed with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and had access to a personal [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. Reyn could also read, write, and speak [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].<ref name="Poly " />

==Behind the scenes==
Adria Reyn was created for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] article ''[[Ord Vaxal: Prison Planet of the Empire]]'', written by [[Gary Astleford]] and published in [[Polyhedron 165|''Polyhedron'' 165]] in [[2003]]. She was illustrated by artist [[James Ryman]]. Astleford suggests a number of ways that Ord Vaxal could be used in a role-playing campaign, such as having the Imperial player characters be tasked with seeking out evidence of Reyn's corruption.<ref name="Poly " />
